It appeared preferable to freight vessels at New York and to load them with the iron in bars; no less than sixty-eight vessels of 1,000 tons were required, quite a fleet, which on May 3rd left New York, took the Ocean route, coasted the American shores, entered the Bahama Channel, doubled the point of Florida, and on the 10th of the same month entered the Bay of Espiritu-Santo and anchored safely in the port of Tampa Town. description: 'A Jules Verne novel in which five castaways build a colony on an uncharted Pacific island.'

*The Mysterious Island* is Jules Verne's great robinsonade, in which five prisoners of the American Civil War escape by balloon and are marooned on an uncharted Pacific island they name [[Lincoln Island]]. Led by the engineer [[Cyrus Harding]], the castaways rebuild civilization from raw nature, aided at intervals by an unseen benefactor who is finally revealed as [[Captain Nemo]]. The novel serves as a sequel that ties together the worlds of *[[Twenty Thousand Leagues Under the Sea]]* and *In Search of the Castaways*.
description: 'A Jules Verne novel in which Professor Liedenbrock descends an Icelandic volcano toward the center of the earth.'
